stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 3 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability of 91%. test 2 has a reliability between 69% and 85% (uncertain due to limited operational data). test 3 has a reliability of 86%. What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9961 to 0.9981. Point estimate: 0.9971.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.31 × 0.14 = 0.0039. P(works) = 0.9961.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.15 × 0.14 = 0.0019. P(works) = 0.9981.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
